reviews.review_description:
Sometimes a game comes along with no bells or whistles and tbe majority miss out on an undiscovered gem, 
This is no ordinary airplane adventure, this is a history lesson in aviation.
Loved the crisp cartoon graphics which really fit the game, I would love some free movement if possible, I did struggle a little with the point & click system,  nothing game breaking but the option to freely move around would really enhance the the gameplay where needed, in the hanger.
The puzzles are clever & never too taxing, and enjoyed the route making staying under budget.
This so far is more a management game than a flying game so far, that is the beauty of this game.
A wonderful concept and a great story to go with it.
This is one I want to keep playing to see where the story goes.
Well done to the dev team a very good entry to meta quest.

I went into Sky Legends VR expecting a simple flying game, but it ended up being way more immersive than I thought. What really stood out to me was the atmosphere. Flying those old-school planes through storms and rough weather in VR genuinely feels intense, especially when you’re relying on manual controls and navigation instead of modern tech.

The game does a great job making you feel like an actual pilot from the early aviation era. I also liked that it wasn’t just nonstop flying missions. There’s exploration, story moments, and interactive sections that help keep things fresh instead of repetitive.

Visually, the environments look great in VR and some of the scenery is honestly stunning when you’re up in the air. The sound design also adds a lot to the immersion with the engine noise, wind, and radio chatter.

That said, the game definitely has a few rough edges. Some controls felt awkward at times and certain interactions could be smoother. The pacing is slower than most VR games too, so people expecting arcade-style action might not connect with it as much.

Overall though, I really enjoyed it. Sky Legends VR feels unique compared to a lot of other VR titles because it focuses more on immersion and adventure than constant action. If you like aviation, exploration, or cinematic VR experiences, it’s absolutely worth checking out. The key thing to remember here is this is a game that takes you on a great journey it’s not a flight sim.

reviews.review_title: Came for Top Gun - learnt how to clean.
reviews.review_description:
This wasn’t what I expected, but I’m still really enjoying it. The graphics are cartoon-styled and look great — really crisp and clear in VR. It’s much more of a narrative, story-driven game than I anticipated, with a few fun puzzles along the way, like mixing cleaning products correctly and working out the best streamlined flying routes to save money.

I did find moving around the hangar between positions a bit clunky and hit-and-miss, sometimes taking several button presses to move where I wanted. That could just be user error though, as I haven’t really seen others mention it in reviews.

Also, devs… I love the wristwatch displaying the actual local time — such a small detail, but a brilliant touch!

Hey, I just wanted to leave a comment because I think the developers have magnificently succeeded in creating an experience that immerses us in the era of the Aéropostale. I believe they found the best way to tell a story while still delivering an enjoyable experience to play, both through stunning visuals in a unique style and through gameplay rich in puzzles, with well-executed flying sequences! I truly recommend this game to anyone who wants to immerse themselves in the fascinating history of the Aéropostale!

It is an amazing app where you can experience all the history of the era of civil aviation development firsthand. It must be carried out with a few puzzle elements, from airplane maintenance, route development while flying on a plane to aviation company management. Manual flights conducted while observing the ground by sticking your head out directly from a plane without radar or HUD seem to be a nice experiences of that era.
Although there are occasional lack of clear description for progress or dull physical interactions, it is not inconvenient to proceed while feeling the overall story and atmosphere.
Merci Lionel!

To Dev, many thanks for Korean subtitle but the fonts are broken and almost characters are shown as just squares.

SKY LEGENDS is a narrative masterpiece that immerses you in the golden age of aviation—a tribute to the daring pioneers of the 1920s who shaped civil aviation. This isn’t just a flight sim; it’s a historical journey where you pilot vintage planes, solve clever puzzles, and navigate a world of technical hurdles, financial struggles, and natural challenges that mirror the real obstacles these heroes faced.
The attention to detail is staggering—from quirky Easter eggs hidden in every corner to the vibrant, meticulously crafted graphics that bring history to life. Whether you’re charting a course through turbulent skies or uncovering the stories behind aviation’s unsung legends, every moment feels authentic and thrilling.
A bold, original experience that honors the past while delivering endless discovery. Buckle up—your adventure awaits! 🛩️

Sky Legends is an absolute triumph for VR gaming. From the moment you put on the headset, you are treated to an excellent and distinct graphic style that immediately sets it apart from other titles in the medium.

What truly elevates the experience is the level of immersion. The interactions with in-game objects feel incredibly fluid and natural, making the world feel tangible and responsive. For those looking for maximum realism, the game features seamless compatibility with the ProTas Joystick, which takes the controls to a whole new level.

Tying it all together is a captivating narrative story that beautifully plunges the player straight into the atmospheric charm of the 1920s. 

Highly recommended!

Sky Legends is a beautiful and immersive VR experience that brings aviation history to life. The hand tracking works great, and the storytelling is engaging and educational. A must-try for anyone who enjoys narrative VR experiences. A hidden Gem!

reviews.review_title: Not just flying but a great story
reviews.review_description: A great game,not just a flying game but a history lesson on aviation. The cartoon style graphics suit it perfectly along with the sounds, background radio playing etc also fit well.some good puzzles but its the story that makes it really interesting. Got me intrigued im now online lookin up these sky legends! Have only played a few chapters of it, looking forward to see how it goes. Would definitely recommend it.

Let's get this out of the way:  this is not what most people would consider a "flight sim," and they are careful not to advertise it as such.  Instead, this is a fantastically atmospheric trip through a specific niche of history.  While aviation is at the core of the story, flying doesn't "feel" like the core of gameplay.  Instead it's the wealth of ancillary activities and puzzles that round out the "game."  

I found the interface to be a little quirky and non-traditional, but you can get past that fairly quickly.  The visual presentation is really nice.  Give this one a chance, but keep your mind open.

L'app, c'est très magnifique. Cette app parler bien sur et août L'histoire de Aeropostale de pays France. Peut-être vous besoin achetez cette app!

The app is great, really great. The app talks well on and abiut the history of air mail in France. Perhaps you need to get this app!

Exquisite painterly watercolour graphics. Among the best of all VR apps. Lots of ingenious escape room puzzles that a walkthrough video will help with. So far, I only completed chapter one, je pense.

Whether you are wealthy rich or weather you are a peasant without pants, un sans coupants, You should be able to afford this app which is a bit pricey.

Antoine St Exupery wrote The Little Prince.

"Bitte, zeichne mir eine Schaf."
